summary: Medical imaging ML researcher at Telkom University (3rd year, GPA 4.0/4.0). Built a bone scan diagnostic pipeline achieving patient-level AUC 0.956, surpassing two commercially deployed clinical systems. First author on an IEEE Xplore conference paper.

experience.role: Research Assistant, Multi-Task CXR Foundation Model
highlights: Developing a hierarchical pathology classifier covering 20+ disease categories with fallback uncertainty estimation for ambiguous cases, aligned to Indonesian healthcare organization standards.
Validated across multiple public clinical datasets. Pathology classifier and organ segmentation module deployed as microservices consumed by the team's web and desktop apps.
Designed MultiscaleTCN from scratch on real mining fleet dashcam footage from BIB, PAMA MTBU, and SIS. Macro F1 0.78, an 11% gain over the model in production.
Deployed a 3-service async cloud-review API with risk escalation rules and full test coverage, verified against the live company MDVR server.
Screened and assessed 212+ papers using Zotero and Parsifal, applying PRISMA-aligned quality assessment and data extraction to produce a structured synthesis mapping publication trends by year.
Recruited, taught, and evaluated 29 students across weekly sessions and a final project.
Supervised 15 project groups from proposal to final demonstration, evaluating technical and scientific quality at each stage.
Led the branch team at YESIST12 Maker Fair.
Identified and distributed competition opportunities across IEEE and BELMAWA programs.

experience.role: Assistant Lecturer, Programming and Algorithms II
highlights: Reviewed 200+ quiz questions with course lecturers, correcting errors before release.
Supported 40+ students with real-time debugging during live programming assessments.
Tutored 40+ students across weekly sessions. Graded four major assessments.

publications.authors: Ema Rachmawati, M. Rifqi Dzaky Azhad et al.
publications.title: Deep Learning-Based Segmentation of Whole-Body Bone Scan Images Using nnU-Netv2
publications.venue: International Journal of Intelligent Engineering and Systems (IJIES-INASS)

publications.title: Deep Learning for Bird Identification Using Sound: Enhancing Avian Monitoring and Conservation in Indonesia and Malaysia

publications.title: From Segmentation to Biomarker Quantification: A Deep Learning Framework for Metastases Detection in Bone Scans

publications.authors: Ema Rachmawati, M. Rifqi Dzaky Azhad
publications.title: Whole-Body Bone Scintigraphy Segmentation Masks: Ground-Truth Annotations for Bone Area Segmentation

projects.title: Bone Metastasis Diagnosis Framework
highlights: Hotspot detection with YOLOv8: mAP@0.5 = 0.599. Skeletal segmentation with nnU-Net v2 across 12 anatomical regions: IoU 0.879.
Patient-level AUC 0.956 with XGBoost and LightGBM, surpassing EXINI (0.83–0.88) and BONENAVI (0.84–0.89), both in clinical use at the time.

projects.title: Deep Learning for Bird Sound Identification
highlights: Fine-tuned BirdNET v2.4 with MixIT source separation on 12,511 recordings across 219 endemic and endangered species in Indonesia, Malaysia, and Borneo.
Macro F1 improved 44.1% over baseline on the endemic subset. Field-validated at Bandung Zoo and Malaysia National Zoo under uncontrolled conditions.

projects.title: Multimodal Deepfake Detection
highlights: One model detecting both video and audio manipulation without attack-type labels at inference time.
Intra-dataset F1 0.94. Cross-dataset F1 0.60 after domain-adversarial training on 223,445 segments from PolyglotFake, FakeAVCeleb, HiDF, CelebDFv2, and FF++.

projects.title: Telplastina: Multi-Task CXR Analysis Platform
highlights: Led pathology classification and organ segmentation within a 5-task clinical CXR platform also covering report generation, VQA, and visual grounding.
Both modules deployed as microservices serving the team's web app and desktop app through shared API endpoints.
